title: ENDPOINT_COUNTER test is racy under parallel test execution
description: In client.rs lines 328-338, the test `connect_with_failover_increments_counter` reads a global `AtomicUsize` (ENDPOINT_COUNTER), calls `connect_with_failover`, reads it again, and asserts the difference is exactly 1. Because Rust unit tests run in parallel by default and ENDPOINT_COUNTER is a process-wide static, any other test that calls `connect_with_failover` concurrently (including `connect_with_failover_empty_list_errors` and `connect_with_failover_increments_counter` itself if re-run) can increment the counter between the two reads, making the assertion non-deterministically fail. Fix: use `#[serial_test::serial]` on tests that read/write ENDPOINT_COUNTER, or restructure to not test the counter value directly (e.g., test observable behavior instead of internal counter state).
